behaviors.reasoning: Correct staleness detection depends on real git/PR state from external commands/services rather than mocked pure logic only.
Session bootstrap warning behavior requires real hook/script execution context and environment interaction.
Mapping an already computed state object to status labels is pure local logic.
Rendering stale status in `/kaizen-wip` against live repo/PR data requires real command and environment boundaries.
Avoiding stale false positives for open/merged PRs requires querying real PR state boundaries.
Rebase/squash correctness requires validating behavior against real git history transformations and branch state.
